_meta.description: Public-facing user journeys that do NOT require Clerk authentication. These are distinct from admin/dashboard flows.
sources: research-hub/PROJECT_HANDOFF.md — Public flows in navigation graph

journeys.name: Conference Registration
journeys.description: A delegate visits the landing page and registers for a conference via the public registration form.

steps.expected: Landing page loads with event info and Register CTA
Registration form loads with all required fields
Form accepts input, phone normalized to E.164

deferred_items: D7: Terms & Privacy page linked from M07 — not yet designed

journeys.name: Faculty Session Confirmation
journeys.description: A faculty member receives a notification and confirms or declines their session assignment.

steps.expected: M55 loads with session details and Confirm/Decline buttons
Session name, date, time (IST), role/responsibilities displayed

journeys.note: Faculty confirm page is accessed via a tokenized link, not Clerk auth. Exact token mechanism NEEDS_HUMAN_DECISION if not documented.

journeys.name: Landing Page Browse
journeys.description: A visitor browses the conference landing page for information without registering.
invariants_checked: INV-001 (eventId scoping — shows only the target event's data)
deferred_items: D5: Speaker Profile expandable card — not yet designed

open_questions.question: How is the faculty confirmation link tokenized? JWT, one-time token, signed URL?
Does the public registration form support multiple events or is it scoped to one event via URL?
What happens after successful registration? Redirect to confirmation page, or inline success message?
